mechanism: Inhibits FLT3, RAF, VEGFR, and PDGFR kinases to block proliferative signaling
additional_notes: Strong FLT3-ITD AML activity; widely used in preclinical MOLM-13 studies

mechanism: Inhibits FLT3, KIT, and PKC to suppress oncogenic kinase signaling
additional_notes: Also known as PKC412; approved for FLT3-mutant AML; active in MOLM-13; IC50 typically ~10–100 nM in FLT3-ITD lines

mechanism: Potently and selectively inhibits FLT3 signaling to induce apoptosis in FLT3-ITD AML cells
additional_notes: Also known as AC220; IC50 often <5 nM in FLT3-ITD lines such as MOLM-13 and MV4-11

mechanism: Inhibits FLT3 and AXL receptor tyrosine kinases to block survival and proliferation signaling
additional_notes: Also known as ASP2215; approved for relapsed/refractory FLT3-mutant AML; robust activity in MOLM-13

mechanism: Inhibits SRC, ABL, KIT, and PDGFR family kinases to disrupt oncogenic signaling
additional_notes: Reported anti-proliferative effects in subsets of AML; some FLT3-ITD lines show sensitivity; IC50 often ~10–200 nM in sensitive lines

mechanism: Inhibits BCR-ABL and KIT tyrosine kinases to reduce leukemic cell proliferation
additional_notes: Modest activity in some AML models, especially KIT- or ABL-dependent; may synergize with other agents

mechanism: Selectively inhibits anti-apoptotic protein BCL-2 to promote mitochondrial apoptosis
additional_notes: Also known as ABT-199; approved in AML with HMA or LDAC; potent activity in MOLM-13; IC50 frequently 5–200 nM in BCL-2–dependent AML

mechanism: Inhibits the delta isoform of PI3K to attenuate PI3K/AKT survival signaling
additional_notes: PI3K/AKT signaling is active in AML; idelalisib has shown preclinical anti-AML activity; expect µM-range effects

mechanism: Irreversibly inhibits Bruton's tyrosine kinase (BTK) to impair B-cell receptor–related signaling pathways
additional_notes: BTK involvement in AML signaling; reported to reduce proliferation in some AML lines and stem/progenitor cells

mechanism: Inhibits JAK1 and JAK2 to block JAK/STAT-mediated cytokine and growth factor signaling
additional_notes: JAK/STAT hyperactivation in some AML subsets; preclinical suppression of AML proliferation and cytokine-driven survival

mechanism: Inhibits MEK1 and MEK2 in the RAS/RAF/MEK/ERK pathway to reduce proliferative signaling
additional_notes: RAS/RAF/MEK pathway active in many AMLs; MEK inhibition can impair proliferation and sensitize to other agents including FLT3 inhibitors; IC50 often in low-nM to 100-nM range

mechanism: Inhibits mTORC1 to reduce protein synthesis and cell growth signaling
additional_notes: Also known as RAD001; mTOR signaling drives AML proliferation; inhibits growth of several AML lines and leukemic stem cells

mechanism: Inhibits multiple histone deacetylases leading to increased histone acetylation, apoptosis, and cell cycle arrest
additional_notes: Also known as LBH589; strong pro-apoptotic/anti-proliferative activity in AML including FLT3-ITD; often synergistic with FLT3/BCL-2 inhibitors; IC50 typically 5–100 nM

mechanism: Inhibits histone deacetylases to promote chromatin relaxation, differentiation, and apoptosis
additional_notes: Also known as SAHA; broad anti-AML activity; induces differentiation and apoptosis in multiple AML lines; IC50 frequently ~0.5–5 µM

mechanism: Incorporates into RNA and DNA and inhibits DNMTs, leading to DNA hypomethylation and gene re-expression
additional_notes: Standard of care in AML/MDS; in vitro anti-proliferative and differentiation effects in AML cells including FLT3-ITD lines; requires longer exposure (72–120 h)

mechanism: Incorporates into DNA and irreversibly traps DNMTs, causing DNA hypomethylation and apoptosis/differentiation
additional_notes: DNMT inhibitor similar to azacitidine; widely used AML drug; reduces proliferation and induces apoptosis/differentiation; 72–120 h exposure recommended

mechanism: Binds cereblon to modulate substrate degradation, reduces TNF-α, and has anti-angiogenic and immunomodulatory effects
additional_notes: Limited clinical AML efficacy but preclinical data show effects on AML cell survival and microenvironment; often more useful in combination therapy; wide concentration range, higher µM usually needed

mechanism: Cereblon-modulating agent that alters ubiquitination of transcription factors and exerts immunomodulatory and anti-proliferative effects
additional_notes: Some activity in AML/MDS with specific genetic contexts such as del(5q); moderate direct cytotoxicity in vitro

mechanism: Activates AMPK and inhibits mitochondrial complex I, inducing energetic stress and secondary mTOR inhibition
additional_notes: Multiple reports of anti-leukemic effects via metabolic stress and mTOR inhibition; often requires higher µM–mM concentrations in vitro; suggested points: 0.1, 0.3, 1, 3, 10, 20 mM

mechanism: Inhibits HMG-CoA reductase to block mevalonate pathway and cholesterol/isoprenoid synthesis, impairing leukemic cell survival
additional_notes: Repurposed statin; mevalonate pathway crucial for leukemic cell survival; statins show anti-AML activity in vitro and synergy with cytotoxic agents; Simvastatin active acid form: 0.1–20 µM mentioned as alternative
